MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT .
Off Calendar Jun-01-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT .

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's ("City") motion for summary judgment is denied.

There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the City owned or controlled the tree which caused the injury. While some of Mr. Comisky's deposition testimony suggests that the City did not own or control the tree that injured Mr. Comisky (e.g. Ex. 5a attached to Mr. Comisky's depo.), other deposition testimony and discovery responses suggest that it did. (See Rogers Decl., Ex. R [Mrs. Comisky Depo.], 37:2-23; Exs. D, E, G; Ex Q [Mr. Comisky Depo.] 61:3-7; 62:19-25; 63:21-64:7; 139:9-13; UMF 7.) Plaintiffs' interrogatory responses identify the Mountain Lake property near Lake Street as the area where the tree was and there is no authority holding that this evidence is inadmissible because plaintiff-allegedly because of confusion-identified a different area at his deposition. Indeed, the City's reply brief concedes that plaintiff timely changed this testimony. The Court excludes the City's reply evidence since it was on notice based on the special interrogatory responses that the Mountain Lake property was where the tree was located. The City should have presented the reply evidence with the moving papers. In any event, there is evidence to suggest that the City owned or controlled the Mountain Lake property. (Rogers Decl., Exs. D, E, G.)

There is also a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the City had actual notice of the dangerous condition. (Rogers Decl., Exs. L, M, N.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC3.1312. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer: W. Trupek, Not reported (302/RBU)
